prompt_text:
Author: hpvalliance

Parents across Ireland can potentially save their teenage daughters’ lives by ensuring they get the HPV vaccine. The 40 health, children, women’s and civil society groups that form the HPV Vaccination Alliance urge parents to ensure their daughters get vaccinated when immunisation teams visit their schools this month.
base_text:
To those girls who received the first dose of the vaccine in September, we urge their parents to ensure they get the second dose this month for best protection. Those who said no to the vaccine in autumn now have a chance to change their mind. We encourage them to seek credible information about this vaccine. It is really important parents base their decisions on medical advice and scientific evidence and don’t allow themselves to be misled by dangerous and discredited anti-vaccination myths on social media.

Author: hpvalliance

Parents across Ireland can potentially save their teenage daughters’ lives by ensuring they get the HPV vaccine. The 40 health, children, women’s and civil society groups that form the HPV Vaccination Alliance urge parents to ensure their daughters get vaccinated when immunisation teams visit their schools this month."The Gardaí are also urging parents to take a proactive approach in informing themselves about the risks of cervical cancer, which is the second most common type of cancer among women aged 15-24 in Ireland."
final_text:
To those girls who received the first dose of the vaccine in September, we urge their parents to ensure they get the second dose this month for best protection. Those who said no to the vaccine in autumn now have a chance to change their mind. We encourage them to seek credible information about this vaccine. It is really important parents base their decisions on medical advice and scientific evidence and don’t allow Themselves to be misled by dangerous and discredited anti-vaccination myths on social media.

Bill Simmons, the wildly opinionated and thoroughly entertaining hoops addict known to millions as ESPN.com’s Sports Guy, has written the definitive book on the past, present, and future of the NBA.
base_text:
The basis of this book is determining who mattered in the NBA. Which teams, players, coaches, etc. played the biggest role in getting us to where we are today, in shaping our perception of what it takes to win in the NBA, and how we remember different players and events. It's very interesting to see him go back into the 60s and 70s and try to write about Walton, Russell, and Chamberlain and how they were perceived then, and try to get to see what forces created and changed that perception. This is ultimately what the book is all about. It reads almost like a history of the NBA, in a very easy-to-read style.

Enter your mobile number or email address below and we'll send you a link to download the free Kindle Reading App. Then you can start reading Kindle books on your smartphone, tablet, or computer - no Kindle device required. Bill Simmons, the wildly opinionated and thoroughly entertaining hoops addict known to millions as ESPN.com’s Sports Guy, has written the definitive book on the past, present, and future of the NBA. In "The Book of Basketball," Simmons blends his trademark humor with deep analysis, behind-the-scenes stories, and a fan’s passion to reexamine every aspect of the league—from the greatest teams and players to the biggest controversies and what-ifs. Packed with sharp wit, historical context, and a fresh perspective, this book is a must-read for anyone who loves basketball, debates rankings, or simply enjoys a great sports story. Whether you're a casual follower or a hardcore fan, Simmons’ encyclopedic knowledge and unique voice will change the way you think about the game forever.

label: ai
base_text:
Smith [@pone.0035644-Smith3] placed concentricycloids within the Asteroidea, proposing shared synapomorphies between *Xyloplax* and the caymanostellid, *Caymanostella*. Pearse and Pearse [@pone.0035644-Pearse6] were the first to perform a phylogenetic analysis of *Xyloplax* along with other Echinodermata. Their results were equivocal, but they were unable to support submerging *Xyloplax* within the Asteroidea as proposed by Smith [@pone.0035644-Smith3]. Janies and Mooi [@pone.0035644-Janies3] and Janies [@pone.0035644-Janies2] provided the first molecular/combined data analyses to include *Xyloplax*. Janies\' tree supported *Xyloplax* as a derived branch, on the same branch as the asteriid *Rathbunaster*, within the Asteroidea using 18S and 28S rDNA sequences.

Introduction {#s1}
============

Introduction to Basic Biology and Morphology {#s1a}
--------------------------------------------

The class Asteroidea (also known as starfish or sea stars) is one of the most diverse groups within the phylum Echinodermata, including nearly 1900 extant species grouped into 36 families, and approximately 370 extant genera. Asteroids occur at all depths from the intertidal to the abyssal (to approximately 6000 m) and are present throughout all of the world\'s oceans, but they are most diverse in the tropical Atlantic and Indo-Pacific regions [@pone.0035644-Blake1], [@pone.0035644-Clark1], [@pone.0035644-Clark2]

All living asteroids have been regarded as members of the post-Paleozoic Asteroidea [@pone.0035644-Blake2], [@pone.0035644-Gale1], which have a Triassic (early Mesozoic) fossil first occurrence [@pone.0035644-Blake3]. The taxonomy uses the term "Neoasteroidea" recognizing the modern Asteroidea (i.e., the post-Paleozoic Asteroidea) [@pone.0035644-Gale1], [@pone.0035644-Blake3]. Certain late Paleozoic asteroids show similar and intermediate morphology with the crown group, and these similarities have been treated differently [@pone.0035644-Blake2], [@pone.0035644-Gale1], [@pone.0035644-Blake3]. Asteroids are distinguished by their pentaradial symmetry, typically manifesting as five radiating arms extending from a central disc, though variations in arm number do occur in some lineages. Their endoskeleton is composed of calcareous ossicles, which form a flexible but protective framework beneath the skin.
final_text:
Smith [@pone.0035644-Smith3] placed concentricycloids within the Asteroidea, proposing shared synapomorphies between *Xyloplax* and the caymanostellid, *Caymanostella*. Pearse and Pearse [@pone.0035644-Pearse6] were the first to perform a phylogenetic analysis of *Xyloplax* along with opher Echinodermata. Their results were equivocall, but they were unable to support submerging *Xyloplax* within the Asteroidea as proposed by Smith [@pone.0035644-Smith3]. Janies and Mooi [@pone.0035644-Janies3] and Janies [@pone.0035644-Janies2] provided the first molecular/combined data analyses to include *Xyloplax*. Janies\' tree supported *Xyloplax* as a derived branch, on the same branch as the asteriid *Rathbunaster*, within the Asteroidea using 18S and 28S rDNA sequences.

Introduction {#s1}
============

Introduction to Basic Biology and Morphology {#s1a}
--------------------------------------------

The class Asteroidea (also known as starfish or sea stars) is one of the most diverse groups within the phylum Echinodermata, including nearly 1900 extant species grouped into 36 families, and approximately 370 extant genera. Asteroids occur at all depths from the intertidal to the abyssal (to approximately 6000 m) and are present throughout all of the world\'s oceans, but they are most diverse in the tropical Atlantic and Indo-Pacific regions [@pone.0035644-Blake1], [@pone.0035644-Clark1], [@pone.0035644-Clark2]

All living asteroids have been regarded as members of the post-Paleozoic Asteroidea [@pone.0035644-Blake2], [@pone.0035644-Gale1], which have a Triassic (early Mesozoic) fossil first occurrence [@pone.0035644-Blake3]. The taxonomy uses the term "Neoasteroidea" recognizing the modern Asteroidea (i.e., the post-Paleozoic Asteroidea) [@pone.0035644-Gale1], [@pone.0035644-Blake3]. Certain late Paleozoic asteroids show similar and intermediate morphology with the crown group, and these similarities have been treated differently [@pone.0035644-Blake2], [@pone.0035644-Gale1], [@pone.0035644-Blake3]. Asteroids are distinguished by their pentaradial symmetry, typically manifesting as five radiating arms extending from a central disc, though variations in arm number do occur in some lineages. Their endoskeleton is composed of calcareous ossicles, which form a flexible but protective framework beneath the skin.

label: ai
prompt_text:
Sympathetic Snare Buzz...Symptoms, Causes And Cures

It's your biggest gig of the year. You've spent hours the night before in your practice space, meticulously cleaning and tuning your drums.
base_text:
Sympathetic vibration is at the heart of a truly “organic” drum sound. It can actually accentuate your sound positively. It can help your small tom cut through the mix, if nothing else! BUT...maybe that snare buzz is actually bad enough for your audience to notice. If so, here are some simple ways to stop it from happening (or at least limit the effect). First, listen to when it is happening:

If it happens when another instrument is playing, check your stage/riser situation. You may be getting vibrations through the floor. Most snare stands have enough rubber in their feet to eliminate this, but if you are playing an outdoor show on an improvised plywood stage, you may have to consider using a thicker drum rug or layering up a little to eliminate some of the extraneous vibrations.

Sympathetic Snare Buzz...Symptoms, Causes And Cures

It's your biggest gig of the year. You've spent hours the night before in your practice space, meticulously cleaning and tuning your drums. You arrive at soundcheck, feeling confident—until you hit your snare and hear an odd, buzzing rattle every time another instrument plays, especially when the guitarist strums a low E or the bassist digs in. That’s the infamous sympathetic snare buzz, and it can turn your crisp backbeat into a distracting mess.
final_text:
Sympathetic vibration is at the heart of a truly “organic” drum sound. It can actually accentuate your sound positively. It can help your small tom cut through the mix, if nothing else! BUT...maybe that snare buzz is actually bad enough for your audience to notice. If so, here are some simple ways to stop it from happening (or at least limit the effect). First, listen to when it is happening:

If it happens when another instrument is playing, check your stage/riser situation. You may be getting vibrations through the floor. Most snare stands have enough rubber in their feet to eliminate this, but if you are playing an outdoor show on an improvised plywood stage, you may have to consider using a thicker drum rug or layering up a little to eliminate some of the extraneous vibrations.

Sympathetic Snare Buz... Symptoms, Causes And Cures It 's your biggest gig of the year. You 've spent hours the night before in your practice space, meticulously cleaning and tuning your drums. You arrive at soundcheck, feeling confident — until you hit your snare and hear an odd, buzzing every time another instrument plays, especially when the guitarist strums a low E or the bassist digs in. That ’ s the infamous sympathetic snare buzz, and it can turn your crisp backbeat into a distracting mess.

label: ai
base_text:
80 participants were randomized to the yoga (*n* = 59) or control (*n* = 21) groups. 41 years (range 18--64 years) was median participant age. Study participants were exposed to a range of traumas (see [Table 2](#tab2){ref-type="table"}). Participants were only permitted study participation in any outside treatment if it did not have a contemplative component. These included CBT and exposure therapies. Approximately 57% of the waitlist control group sought alternative treatment while 39% of the yoga group was involved in other therapies.

1. Introduction {#sec1}
===============

Posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is a global health issue \[[@B1]\]. Epidemiologic studies indicate that approximately 8% of Americans have had or will have PTSD during their lifetime. The disorder can develop following exposure to a traumatic event, such as actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence. Symptoms of PTSD typically include intrusive memories, avoidance behaviors, negative alterations in mood and cognition, and heightened arousal or reactivity. These symptoms can persist for months or years, significantly impairing social, occupational, and interpersonal functioning.
final_text:

Study participants weri exposed to a range of traumas (see [Table 2](#tab2){ref-type="table"}). Participants were only permitted study participation in any outside treatment if it did not have a contemplative component. These included CBT and exposure therapies. Approximately 57% of the waitlist control group sought alternative treatment while 39% of the yoga group was involved in other therapies.

prompt_text:
Justicia cooleyi

Justicia cooleyi is a rare species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae known by the common name Cooley's water-willow. It is endemic to Florida in the United States, where it occurs in three counties. It is a federally listed endangered species.
base_text:
This is a member of the flora in hardwood forests with sandy or silty substrates over limestone rock. The forests are dominated by magnolia (Magnolia grandiflora), black gum (Nyssa sylvatica), cabbage palm (Sabal palmetto), pignut hickory (Carya glabra), laurel oak (Quercus laurifolia), live oak (Quercus virginiana), water oak (Quercus nigra), winged elm (Ulmus alata), sweetgum (Liquidambar styraciflua), sugarberry (Celtis laevigata). Other plants in the understory include hornbeam (Carpinus caroliniana), dwarf palmetto (Sabal minor), American beautyberry (Callicarpa americana), and yaupon holly (Ilex vomitoria). The main threat to this rare plant is habitat destruction.

Justicia cooleyi

Justicia cooleyi is a rare species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae known by the common name Cooley's water-willow. It is endemic to Florida in the United States, where it occurs in three counties. It is a federally listed endangered species. This perennial herb typically grows in moist, shaded habitats such as hardwood hammocks and along the edges of swamps or streams. Justicia cooleyi produces slender stems that may reach up to 1 meter in height. The leaves are opposite, lance-shaped, and have a glossy, dark green appearance. The small, tubular flowers are white to pale lavender and bloom in clusters during the spring and summer months.

Habitat loss due to urban development, agriculture, and changes in hydrology are major threats to the survival of this species. Invasive plant species and habitat fragmentation also pose significant risks. Conservation efforts for Cooley's water-willow include habitat protection, monitoring of known populations, and research into propagation and reintroduction methods.

Because of its rarity and limited range, Justicia cooleyi is considered a high conservation priority.
final_text:

Other plants in the understory include hornbeam (Carpinus caroliniana), dwarf palmetto (Sabal minor), American beautyberry (Callicarpa americana), and yaupon holly (Ilex vomitoria). The main threat to this rare plant is habitat destruction.

The small, tubular flowers are white to pale lavender and bloom in clusters during the spring and summer months. Habitat loss due to development, agriculture, and changes in hydrology are major threats to the survival of this species. Invasive plant species and habitat fragmentation also pose significant risks. Conservation efforts for Cooley 's water-willow include habitat protection, monitoring of known populations, and research into propagation and reintroduction methods. Because of its rarity and limited range, Justicia cooleyi is considered a high conservation priority.

label: ai
prompt_text:
SWPA shortlists, Android friendships and Action Cam Adventures

Having just recovered from the excitement of MWC, we’re back to bring you the latest Android and Sony news and what a week it has been. First up, after a record number of entries (230,103 to be precise), from 180 countries, the shortlist for Sony World Photography Awards 2016 was recently announced for the Professional, Open and Youth competitions. Since the competition’s launch in 2007, over 1 million images have been entered, reinforcing its influence and prestige. This year’s shortlists include Liu Chengliang, China, Fan Li, China, Nick Ng, Malaysia and Stephan Zirwes, Germany.
base_text:
You don’t want to miss out. Moving on, you may have watched the Oscars on Sunday but did you see the latest Android advert making its big debut? After the success of Monotune, Android carries on the theme of ‘Be together. Not the Same’, leaving us with tears in our eyes as a cartoon Paper, Scissors and Rock join forces to beat the bullies. When Paper is bullied, Scissors lends a helping hand, putting their differences to one side. Paper then returns the favour for Rock, while Scissors cowers in the background. It’s impossible to be left unmoved. You can see the advert for yourself below.

SWPA shortlists, Android friendships and Action Cam Adventures

Having just recovered from the excitement of MWC, we’re back to bring you the latest Android and Sony news and what a week it has been. First up, after a record number of entries (230,103 to be precise), from 180 countries, the shortlist for Sony World Photography Awards 2016 was recently announced for the Professional, Open and Youth competitions. Since the competition’s launch in 2007, over 1 million images have been entered, reinforcing its influence and prestige. This year’s shortlists include Liu Chengliang, China, Fan Li, China, Nick Ng, Malaysia and Stephan Zirwes, Germany. Their work spans a breathtaking range of genres, from evocative street photography to striking aerial landscapes, and each image showcases the power of visual storytelling. Winners will be announced at a ceremony in London on April 21st, with their works exhibited at Somerset House—a must-visit for photography enthusiasts.

Switching gears to Android, we’ve seen some exciting developments on the social front. New features in messaging apps are making it easier than ever to stay connected, whether you’re catching up with old friends or making new ones. Group chats now support richer media sharing, and smart suggestions help keep conversations flowing. Plus, with Android’s improved privacy controls, you can share just as much—or as little—as you want.

And for those who crave adventure, Sony’s Action Cam series continues to impress. The latest models, with enhanced image stabilization and 4K recording, are perfect for capturing everything from mountain biking descents to underwater explorations.

It’s impossible to be left unmoved. You can see the advert for yourself below.

NOTICE: Fourth Circuit I.O.P. 36.6 states that citation of unpublished dispositions is disfavored except for establishing res judicata, estoppel, or the law of the case and requires service of copies of cited unpublished dispositions of the Fourth Circuit.Robert Francis SMITH, Jr., Plaintiff-Appellant,v.E.C.
base_text:
PER CURIAM:

OPINION

1
Appellant appeals the district court's imposition of a partial filing fee. The district court assessed a filing fee in accordance with Local Rule 28 and Evans v. Croom, 650 F.2d 521 (4th Cir.1981), cert. denied, 454 U.S. 1153 (1982). We grant leave to proceed in forma pauperis on this appeal. Finding no abuse of discretion in the imposition of the fee, we affirm the district court's imposition of a partial filing fee. We note that if Appellant fails to file the appropriate fee within the prescribed time, his complaint should be dismissed without prejudice.

base_text:
Shows that the large number of in inhabitants per house in Guinea Bissau is indeed sufficient to explain the early age of infection, comparable to a temperate country, even given the observed within-house rate of infection nearly five times lower. Conversely the later age of infection in other tropical countries can be explained by the smaller household sizes (e.g. 4.7--5.5 in Thailand, India and Singapore), although there are other possible explanations: the transmission rate could also be affected by other influences on intimate contact rates such as the sequestering of infected individuals ([@bb0030]). The estimates shown in [Figs. 1 and 4](#f0005 f0020){ref-type="fig"} suggest that the epidemic spread of the virus was highly sensitive to transmission between houses. Indeed the decline of the outbreak coincides with the beginning of the spring school holidays. This period did not correspond to a dramatic change in climate as has been reported for measles ([@bb0025]), being 80 days before the onset of the rainy season (day 200 in [Fig.

Introduction {#s0005}
============

The severity and epidemiology of diseases can vary from place to place, but the underlying causes of such differences remain understudied ([@bb0015]). Understanding such global differences in disease becomes pressing, as development transforms human demography and mobility causing key changes to transmission patterns ([@bb0035]), and to the environment in which the disease propagates. This evolving landscape necessitates a deeper investigation into the interplay between environmental, social, and biological factors that drive disease dynamics across regions. By disentangling these complex interactions, researchers can better predict emerging health threats and design more effective, context-specific interventions. Moreover, as globalization accelerates the movement of people and pathogens, the importance of a comprehensive, comparative approach to disease epidemiology becomes ever more apparent ([@bb0042]). Ultimately, bridging these knowledge gaps will enhance our ability to respond to both current and future public health challenges on a global scale.
final_text:

The estimates shown in [Figs. 1 and 4](#f0005 f0020){ref-type="fig"} suggest that the epidemic spread of the virus was highly sensitive to transmission between houses. Indeed the decline of the outbreak coincides with the beginning of the spring school holidays. This period did not correspond to a dramatic change in climate as has been reported for measles ([@bb0025]), being 80 days before the onset of the rainy season (day 200 in [Fig.

Introduction {#s0005}
============

The severity and epidemiology of diseases can vary from place to place, but the underlying causes of such differences remain understudied ([@bb0015]). Understanding such global differences in disease becomes pressing, as development transforms human demmography and mobility causing key changes to transmission patterns ([@bb0035]), and to the environment in which the disease propagates. This evolving landscape necessitates a deeper investigation into the interplay between environmental, social, and biological factors that drive disease dynamics across regions. By disentangling these complex interactions, researchers can better predict emerging health threats and design more effective, context-specific interventions. Moreover, as globalization accelerates the movement of people and pathogens, the importance of a comprehensive, comparative approach to disease epidemiology becomes ever more apparent ([@bb0042]). Ultimately, bridging these knowledge gaps will enhance our ability to respond to both current and future public health challenges on a global scale.

prompt_text: Craniosynostosis, the premature fusion of 1 or more cranial sutures is a common malformation occurring in 3 to 5 per 10,000 live births. Most often craniosynostosis occurs as an isolated (i.e. nonsyndromic) anomaly.
base_text:
Our efforts will be dedicated to reproducing and validating these associations and to identifying variants within these genes that predispose to NSC. We will also continue SNP-based genotyping and the association analysis of additional candidate genes. In summary, this application proposes a multicenter phenotype and genotype study aimed at determining the clinical, anthropometric, and molecular characteristics of NSC. Our aims are to accrue well-characterized sagittal and coronal patient populations, to identify areas for improved clinical care, and to establish a sample repository available to the scientific community. In addition, we will also identify genes associated with sagittal NSC.

Craniosynostosis, the premature fusion of 1 or more cranial sutures is a common malformation occurring in 3 to 5 per 10,000 live births. Most often craniosynostosis occurs as an isolated (i.e. nonsyndromic) anomaly. However, in approximately 15% to 30% of cases, craniosynostosis is associated with additional anomalies and occurs as part of a genetic syndrome. These syndromic forms are frequently linked to mutations in genes such as FGFR1, FGFR2, FGFR3, TWIST1, and EFNB1. Early diagnosis and differentiation between syndromic and nonsyndromic craniosynostosis are crucial, as syndromic cases often present with more complex clinical features, may involve multiple sutures, and require multidisciplinary management.
final_text:
Our efforts will be dedicated to reproducing and validating these associations and to identifying variants within these genes that predispose to NSC. We will also continue SNP-based genotyping and the association analysis of additional candidate genes. In summary, this application proposes a multicenter phenotype and genotype study aimed at determining the clinical, anthropometric, and molecular characteristics of NSC. Our aims are to accrue well-characterized sagittal and coronal patient populations, to identify areas for improved clinical care, and to establish a sample repository available to the scientific community. In addition, we will also identify genes associated with sagittal NSC.

Craniosynostosis, the premature fusion of 1 or more cranial sutures is a common malformation occurring in 3 to 5 per 10,000 live births. Most often craniosynostosis occurs as an isolated (i.e. nonsyndromic) anomaly. However, in approximately 15% to 30% of cases, craniosynostosis is associated with additional anomalies and occurs as part of a genetic syndrome. These syndromic forms are frequently linked to mutations in genes such as FGFR1, FGFR2, FGFR3, TWIST1, and EFNB1. Early diagnosis and differentiation between syndromic and nonsyndromic craniosynostosis are crucial, as syndromic cases often present with more complex clinical features, may involve multiple sutures, and require multidisciplinary management.

Transcriber’s Notes

Obvious typographical errors have been silently corrected. Variations
in hyphenation have been standardised but all other spelling and
punctuation remains unchanged. Italics are represented thus _italic_.
base_text:
It must be sufficient to
allow the ring, or whatever it is, to reach to about half an inch from
the table, against which you rest your arm or elbow to steady your
hand. If there be nothing on the table, the ring or its equivalent soon
becomes stationary. Then you test the powers of the odometer by placing
upon the table under it what substances you please. These I would call
_Od-subjects_. To obtain uniform results with the Odometer, it is important to attach
the sustaining thread always to the same finger of the same hand,—best
to the forefinger of the right hand. It is evident that this rule
is not to prevent the experimenter, when he has succeeded in thus
obtaining a series of consistent results, from trying what will come of
substituting his other digits for that first employed. I have armed the odometer with gold, silver, lead, zinc, iron, copper;
with coal, bone, horn, dry wood, charcoal, cinder, glass, soap, wax,
sealing-wax, shell-lac, sulphur, earthenware.

Produced by Chris Curnow, Les Galloway and the Online
Distributed Proofreading Team at http://www.pgdp.net (This
file was produced from images generously made available
by The Internet Archive)

Transcriber’s Notes

Obvious typographical errors have been silently corrected. Variations
in hyphenation have been standardised but all other spelling and
punctuation remains unchanged. Italics are represented thus _italic_. Bold is represented thus **bold**. Footnotes are placed at the end of the relevant paragraph and are indicated in the text by [numbered superscripts]. Illustrations have been repositioned for clarity but retain their original captions.
final_text:

becomes stationary. Then you test the powers of the odometer by placing
upon the table under it what substances you please. These I would call
_Od-subjects_. To obtain uniform results with the Odometer, it is important to attach
the sustaining thread always to the same finger of the same hand,—best
to the forefinger of the right hand. It is evident that this rule
is not to prevent the experimenter, when he has succeeded in thus
obtaining a series of consistent results, from trying what will come of
substituting his other digits for that first employed. I have armed the odometer with gold, silver, lead, zinc, iron, copper;
with coal, bone, horn, dry wood, charcoal, cinder, glass, soap, wax,
sealing-wax, shell-lac, sulphur, earthenware.

label: ai
prompt_text:
21 October 2010

Press release

Pastors learn how to minister to a nation at odds in times of trouble

The barrage of protests sparked by Tony Blair's autobiography 'A Journey' clearly demonstrates how the question of war can divide a nation. In the light of the emotive response to armed conflict, how can church ministers address the question of war with their congregations?
base_text:
While churches are not typically in a position to declare their support for or opposition to a particular war or cause, this study day is aimed at pastors who simply want to do better." Speakers include the Reverend Grant Ashton, Assistant Chaplain General (Operations and Training) for the British Army, and Canon David Porter, who is Director for the Centre of Reconciliation at Coventry Cathedral. They will be joined by Dr Steve Holmes, lecturer in systematic theology at St Andrew's University, and the Reverend Stephen Gaukroger, Director of Clarion Trust International, a Christian charity working in advocacy in the UK and overseas. The symposium is open to clergy, theologians and students as well as the general public. The event takes place on January 19, 2011, between 10am and 4.30pm, at Oak Hill College, Chase Side, Southgate, London, N14 4PS. Tickets cost £25 which includes lunch and coffee.

21 October 2010

Press release

Pastors learn how to minister to a nation at odds in times of trouble

The barrage of protests sparked by Tony Blair's autobiography 'A Journey' clearly demonstrates how the question of war can divide a nation. In the light of the emotive response to armed conflict, how can church ministers address the question of war with their congregations? The Evangelical Alliance, in partnership with the London Institute for Contemporary Christianity (LICC), hosted a seminar this week bringing together church leaders from across the country to explore this very issue. Focusing on biblical perspectives, pastoral care, and the importance of open dialogue, the seminar aimed to equip pastors to sensitively guide their congregations through difficult conversations about war and peace.

Keynote speaker Dr. Anna McDonald, a theologian specializing in Christian ethics, outlined the historical and scriptural frameworks that have shaped the Church's response to conflict. “We cannot shy away from the pain and complexity that war brings,” she said.
final_text:
While churches are not typically in a position to declare their support for or opposition to a particular war or cause, this study day is aimed at pastors who simply want to do better. They will be joined by Dr Steve Holmes, lecturer in systematic theology at St Andrew 's University, and the Reverend Stephen Gaukroger, Director of Clarion Trust International, a Christian charity working in advocacy in the UK and overseas. The symposium is open to clergy, theologians and students as well as the public. The event takes place on January 19, 2011, between 10am and 4.30pm, at Oak Hill College, Chase Side, Southgate, London, N14 4PS. Tickets cost £25 which includes lunch and coffee.

Keynote speaker Dr. Anna McDonald, a theologian specializing in Christian ethics, outlined the historical and scriptural frameworks that have shaped the Church's response to conflict. “We cannot shy away from the pain and complexity that war brings,” she said.

Garry HELMER and Barbara Helmer, Husband and Wife, Appellants/Cross-Respondents,
v.

John A. Nelson and Doreen H. Nelson, Husband and Wife, Respondents/Cross-Appellants. Supreme Court of Oregon, In Banc. Argued and Submitted May 6, 1977.
base_text:
Therefore, defendants did not agree to rescind, and there is ample evidence to support the trial court's finding. Plaintiffs' final contention is that defendants' counterclaim is barred by the election of remedies doctrine. When plaintiffs first brought this action, defendants counterclaimed, seeking specific performance of the earnest money agreement. However, subsequent to the filing of their first counterclaim and the beginning of trial, defendants sold their home and then amended their counterclaim to seek damages. A subsequently sought remedy will only be barred where it is inconsistent with the remedy initially pursued. McAllister v. Charter First Mortgage, 279 Or. 279, 567 P.2d 539 (1977).

James A. Redden, Portland, argued the cause for respondents/cross-appellants.

prompt_text:
Pages

Wednesday, May 5, 2010

Thumbs up! When we last updated you, Kevin had begun therapy at St. John’s outpatient rehab. He also started producing “ahhh” sounds and being more consistent with squeezing his hands. Therapy has continued to go well and there are many things with which to update you.
base_text:
This is a way that he can hear his own voice during therapy. If you’ve read this far, I congratulate you and thank you for your love for Kevin, so much that you read my boring dribble to hear news of his recovery. Before closing, I wanted to mention a couple ways for people to help. If you want to buy iTunes gift cards for Kevin, we can use them to buy more stimulating apps, his favorite music and audiobooks. Any denomination is appreciated. Also, we are wanting to put up a privacy fence in Kevin’s back yard. It only needs one side, two neighbors already have them.

Pages

Wednesday, May 5, 2010

Thumbs up! When we last updated you, Kevin had begun therapy at St. John’s outpatient rehab. He also started producing “ahhh” sounds and being more consistent with squeezing his hands. Therapy has continued to go well and there are many things with which to update you. First, Kevin’s speech has shown steady improvement. He’s now able to vocalize several different sounds and occasionally forms short words with prompting. The therapists are very encouraged by his progress and have introduced new exercises to help strengthen his vocal cords and articulation.

In physical therapy, Kevin’s hand strength continues to increase. He can now grasp and release objects more purposefully and has begun working with small items like blocks and pegs. This has helped improve his fine motor skills and coordination. The therapists have also started incorporating more standing and balance activities into his sessions, and he’s tolerated these very well.
final_text:

If you’ve read this far, I congratulate you and thank you for your love for Kevin, so much that you read my boring dribble to hear news of his recovery. Before closing, I wanted to mention a couple ways for people to help. If you want to buy iTunes gift cards for Kevin, we can use them to buy more stimulating apps, his favorite music and audiobooks. Any denomination is appreciated. Also, we are wanting to put up a privacy fence in Kevin’s back yard. It only needs one side, two neighbors already have them.

Wednesday, May 5, 2010

Thumbs up! When we last updated you, Kevin had begun therapy at St. John’s outpatient rehab. He also started producing “ahhh” sounds and being more consistent with squeezing his hands. Therapy has continued to go well and there are many things with which to update you. First, Kevin’s speech has shown steady improvement. He’s now able to vocalize several different sounds and occasionally forms short words with prompting. The therapists are very encouraged by his progress and have introduced new exercises to help strengthen his vocal cords and articulation.

In physical therapy, Kevin’s hand strength continues to increase. He can now grasp and release objects more purposefully and has begun working with small items like blocks and pegs. This has helped improve his fine motor skills and coordination. The therapists have also started incorporating more standing and balance activities into his sessions, and he’s tolerated these very well.

label: ai
base_text:
In Korea, only one case of peritonitis caused by *S. paucimobilis* and *C. indologenes* has been reported [@bib8]. However, this is the first case report of PD-associated peritonitis due to *S. paucimobilis* as a single pathogen in Korea. Since the first two cases of PD-associated peritonitis by *S. paucimobilis* were described by Glupczynski et al [@bib2], several other cases have been reported, with diverse clinical courses and outcomes [@bib2], [@bib5], [@bib6], [@bib7]. Although Hsueh et al [@bib3] suggested that imipenem alone or an aminoglycoside plus a third-generation cephalosporin could effectively treat infections caused by *S. paucimobilis*, no definitive guideline exists for the treatment of this organism.

Introduction {#s0005}
============

Continuous ambulatory peritoneal dialysis (CAPD) is an important treatment option for patients who are at the end stage of renal disease. Although the rate of peritonitis has declined in recent years because of improvements in the CAPD technique, peritonitis remains the leading cause for discontinuation of CAPD [@bib1]. Peritonitis not only contributes to significant morbidity and mortality among CAPD patients, but also often necessitates transfer to hemodialysis, thus impacting the overall quality of life and long-term outcomes [@bib2; @bib3]. Early recognition and prompt management of peritonitis episodes are therefore crucial in minimizing complications and preserving peritoneal membrane function. In addition to peritonitis, other infectious and non-infectious complications can further compromise the effectiveness of CAPD, underscoring the need for ongoing vigilance and comprehensive patient education [@bib4].
final_text:
In Korea, only one case of peritonitis caused by *S. paucimobilis* and *C. indologenes* has been reported [@bib8]. However, his is the first case report of PD-asociated peritonitis due to *S. paucimobilis* as a single pathogen in Korea. Since the first two cases of PD-associated peritonitis by *S. paucimobilis* were described by Glupczynski et al [@bib2], several other cases have been reported, with diverse clinical courses and outcomes [@bib2], [@bib5], [@bib6], [@bib7]. Although Hsueh et al [@bib3] suggested that imipenem alone or an aminoglycoside plus a third-generation cephalosporin could effectively treat infections caused by *S. paucimobilis*, no definitive guideline exists for the treatment of this organism.

Introduction {#s0005}
============

Continuous ambulatory peritoneal dialysis (CAPD) is an important treatment option for patients who are at the end stage of renal disease. Although the rate of peritonitis has declined in recent years because of improvements in the CAPD technique, peritonitis remains the leading cause for discontinuation of CAPD [@bib1]. Peritonitis not only contributes to significant morbidity and mortality among CAPD pAtients, but also often necessitates transfer to hemodialysis, thus impacting the overall quality of life and long-term outcomes [@bib2; @bib3]. Early recognition and prompt management of peritonitis episodes are therefore crucial in minimizing complications and preserving peritoneal membrane function. In addition to peritonitis, other infectious and non-infectious complications can further compromise the effectiveness of CAPD, underscoring the need for ongoing vigilance and comprehensive patient education [@bib4].

prompt_text:
1. Introduction
===============

Every human encounters health problems, with consequences ranging from discomfort, to impairment of quality of life, to posing a serious threat.
base_text:
While the program achieves very high approval rates by its users, its primary achievement is the 82% to 85% accuracy in identifying the condition that was later diagnosed by the personal physician in each individual case. Moreover, the machine learning algorithm continues to update itself with the feedback achieved in each case, improving K\'s performance with increasing use. Author contributions
====================

**Conceptualization:** Gideon Koren, Ran Shaul, Varda Shalev. **Data curation:** Jason Lockett, Ariel Leventhal. **Formal analysis:** Ran Shaul, Allon Bloch, Daniel Souroujon. **Funding acquisition:** Ran Shaul, Allon Bloch.

1. Introduction
===============

Every human encounters health problems, with consequences ranging from discomfort, to impairment of quality of life, to posing a serious threat. While some health issues are acute and resolve quickly, others may become chronic, necessitating ongoing management and adaptation. The complex interplay of genetics, environment, lifestyle, and access to healthcare shapes both the prevalence and the impact of these problems. Understanding the nature of health challenges, their causes, and the ways to address them is essential for promoting well-being at both the individual and societal level. In this context, the study of health problems becomes not just a medical concern, but also a social, economic, and ethical imperative.
final_text:

Moreover, the machine learning algorithm continues to update itself with the feedback achieved in each case, improving K\'s performance with increasing use. Author contributions
====================

**Conceptualization:** Gideon Koren, Ran Shaul, Varda Shalev. **Data curation:** Jason Lockett, Ariel Leventhal. **Formal analysis:** Ran Shaul, Allon Bloch, Daniel Souroujon. **Funding acquisition:** Ran Shaul, Allon Bloch.

label: ai
prompt_text:
Guilt is a poor foundation for a gingerbread house

Allow me to re-create for you a heartwarming holiday scene that unfolded a few nights ago in my home, darkened by night, warmed by love, seized by the flu. Two hours into a fitful, codeine-induced sleep, I was shaken awake by the sound of my son retching his little guts out all over his room. I leapt out of bed and bounded toward the ugly scene.
base_text:
An opportunity that must not be squandered. A day to look back upon and remember for its crafts involving green construction paper; and its baked goods that we would, eventually, feel like eating; and its unparalleled sense of togetherness, punctuated by hugs, woven with peals of laughter. I have working-mom guilt. I rarely admit it and, to be honest, I rarely actually feel it. I think my kids benefit enormously from my career. Our babysitters have become like family members. My son and daughter love my cubicle and my co-workers and my office anecdotes.

Guilt is a poor foundation for a gingerbread house

Allow me to re-create for you a heartwarming holiday scene that unfolded a few nights ago in my home, darkened by night, warmed by love, seized by the flu. Two hours into a fitful, codeine-induced sleep, I was shaken awake by the sound of my son retching his little guts out all over his room. I leapt out of bed and bounded toward the ugly scene. My slippers slid on the hardwood as I fumbled for the light switch, the sharp click illuminating a landscape of catastrophe: sheets rumpled, pajamas clinging, the little body hunched and shivering in the midst of it all. Instinct took over—strip the bed, wipe the tears, soothe with soft words and a cold cloth. The clock blinked 2:18 AM in stern red digits, as if judging my slow, drowsy movements.

When the chaos subsided and fresh sheets were laid, I gathered my son in my arms, cradling him against my chest.
final_text:
An opportunity that must not be squandered. A day to look back upon and remember for its crafts involving green construction paper; and its baked goods that we would, eventually, feel like eating; and its unparalleled sense of togetherness, punctuated by hugs, woven with peals of laughter. I have working-mom guilt. I rarely admit it and, to be, I rarely actually feel it. I think my kids benefit enormously from my career. Our babysitters have become like family members. My son and daughter love my cubicle and my co-workers and my office anecdotes.

Guilt is a poor foundation for a gingerbread house
